what_it_does: This file is a living, append-only ledger of affordance-grain exclusions for the company/ui-contract atlas: it records features deliberately not exposed as company capabilities, states what each would be, why it is out, what to do instead, and aliases that should out-retrieve lookalike requests.
observations: Front matter marks the file as type contract-out-of-scope, captured 2026-06-12, status living, and shared append-only with lanes adding rows under their own heading.
F6 (Knowledge & memory) has no fully out-of-scope affordances; all touched items are contracted as building or planned.
F8 (Platform & admin) lists seven out-of-local-scope affordances that govern the host install or Anthropic org rather than the company fabric.

A reopened note explains that CC-24.2/.3/.4 (relogin/logout/setup-token) moved from out-of-scope to building because of a sole-operator trust model; what remains out is proxying a different user's credentials.
Each table row includes affordance ID, what it would be, why it is out, what to do instead, aliases, decided-by, and date.
The document repeatedly distinguishes 'planned' (in scope, contracted gap with adoption path) from 'out' (deliberate boundary).
References to other atlas files use [[file#op: ...]] and [[file#anchor]] style links.
standouts: Explicit non-silent exclusions: every out item is paired with an alternative path and aliases.
The reopened CC-24.2/.3/.4 note is a strong signal that scope decisions can be revisited based on operator-trust assumptions.
F8 items are framed as out-of-local-scope because host/org authority outranks the company fabric, not because the capability is impossible.
conventions: Uses contract-out-of-scope frontmatter with living/append-only status.
Organizes exclusions by functional lane (F6, F8) and tabulates them with a fixed schema.
Cross-references related in-scope capabilities via [[...]] links.
Distinguishes 'planned' (in scope) from 'out' (boundary) per referenced contract format sections.
concerns: The CC-01.1 / CC-02.1 row combines two distinct IDs in one table cell, which could complicate automated lookup.
The phrase 'out-retrieves a lookalike' is stated as a goal but the mechanism is not defined in this file.
The reopened note is long and embedded as a blockquote rather than a structured decision row, making it harder to scan.
A line break in the middle of the CONTRACT-FORMAT citation ('§1\natlas/:') looks like a formatting artifact.
notes: A boundary catalog that says 'no' loudly: it lists excluded F8 platform/admin affordances, gives alternatives and aliases, and documents a sole-operator reopening for auth credential acts.
questions: What is the exact content of the referenced files like [[auth#op: auth.act]] and [[platform#op: platform.privacy]]?
What process governs 'reopening' an out-of-scope item, and who can authorize it?
How are the aliases expected to be used at retrieval time — is there a search index, embedding, or prompt injection?
What is 'L-⑤-auto' and what does the '⑤' denote?
Where is the full mapping of all CC-XX IDs to capabilities documented?
What is the precise boundary between the reopened CC-24.2/.3/.4 own-credential acts and any multi-user or shared-credential scenario?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is an affordance-grain out-of-scope ledger with reasons, alternatives, and aliases.
apparent_themes: Host and org authority outrank the company fabric
Sole-operator trust can override default lockdown
Planned gaps are in scope; only deliberate boundaries are out
intention_signals: The file appears designed to prevent scope creep and misrouted requests by making exclusions explicit, navigable, and searchable, while leaving room to revisit boundaries when the operator model changes.
novelty: The combination of aliases-to-out-retrieve-lookalikes and a structured 'reopened' exception based on sole-operator trust, plus the careful semantic distinction between 'planned' (in scope) and 'out' (boundary).
contribution: It contributes a clear, queryable boundary ledger that maps excluded affordances to reasons, alternative paths, and lookalike aliases, and it preserves the nuance that some previously-excluded auth acts were reopened under a sole-operator model.
summary_for_embedding: A living append-only contract file listing F8 platform and admin affordances deliberately out of local scope for the company fabric, with reasons, alternative paths, aliases, and a note reopening CC-24 auth acts under a sole-operator trust model.
intention_for_embedding: To make exclusions explicit and retrievable so that agents and users do not request or build capabilities that belong to the host, org, or Anthropic policy layers.
